meta.document_title: Unlock Decumulation Engine — Technical Specification v1.5
meta.purpose: This document specifies the algorithm, tax logic, data requirements and constraint model for the Unlock Decumulation Planner. It is the primary document sent to LLM reviewers (Perplexity, ChatGPT, Claude) for independent technical feedback before the next development sprint.
meta.author: Unlock — Tom King

project_description.what_unlock_is: Unlock is a UK wealth intelligence platform for high-net-worth private investors (£250k–£5M+ portfolios). It provides institutional-grade portfolio intelligence: an asset register, tax modelling, EIS/VCT deal flow curation, and — via this module — decumulation planning.
project_description.what_the_decumulation_planner_does: The Decumulation Planner answers a single question: given my assets, how long will my money last and in what order should I draw from them? The user provides their income target, plan horizon, lifestyle level, cash reserve floor, and legacy bequest target. Assets flow automatically from the Unlock asset register (cash, ISA, pension, VCTs, EIS direct, AIM shares, investment property). The engine runs a year-by-year simulation and outputs: a funded timeline, portfolio value chart by asset class, IHT trajectory, tax drag summary, net estate after IHT, and a plain-English year-by-year action plan.
project_description.architecture: Local-first. The desktop application is the system of record — all processing runs locally. A cloud layer handles encrypted snapshot sync to a mobile viewer. The decumulation engine is a pure function (no UI or cloud dependencies) that can run on the local Node.js desktop app without modification.
project_description.target_user: UK HNW investor, typically 55–75, self-directed or adviser-informed. Complex multi-asset portfolios including assets IFAs cannot advise on (VCTs, EIS direct). Reference user: retired, widowed, estate of approximately £9M including VCTs, main residence, rental property, ISA, SIPP, and cash.
project_description.regulatory_context: The tool is a planning aid, not regulated financial advice. All outputs are clearly labelled as planning estimates. Two material proposed-but-not-enacted rule changes are modelled as toggleable scenarios with disclosure banners: the April 2026 BPR/APR cap (£2.5M combined) and the April 2027 pension IHT inclusion.
project_description.reference_benchmark: The 2010 Towry Lifetime Financial Plan — a traditional IFA decumulation report. Unlock produces the equivalent dynamically, in real time, covering asset classes IFAs cannot touch.

what_has_been_built.description: A working React/TypeScript prototype has been delivered (v1.0.0, March 2026). This spec reflects both the original design and the features added by the builder. The following were built beyond the original brief:
additions: F001: Weighted multi-objective drawdown priority sliders — four continuous dimensions (Tax Efficiency, IHT Reduction, Preserve Growth, Liquidity) replace the fixed four-strategy enum. Weights auto-normalise. Preset buttons set common profiles. Custom blends compared as a fifth column.

F002: Action Plan — year-by-year plain-English timeline of recommended actions: what to sell, draw, gift; taxes due; milestones; shortfalls; asset depletion events. Key Years / All Years filter modes.
F003: Cash buffer — user sets a minimum cash reserve; engine enforces as hard floor across all cash-class assets.

F004: Legacy target — user specifies net-of-IHT bequest goal. Engine uses this to influence draw sequencing (prefer preserving IHT-efficient assets) and surface LEGACY_FLOOR_AT_RISK warning. Does not hard-block living-cost spending — spend target always takes precedence.
F005: IHT savings vs no-plan baseline — engine computes IHT if no draws or gifts were ever made, compares against actual plan.
F006: Asset depletion visibility — Action Plan detects and marks when any asset class reaches zero.
F007: BPR-aware draw scoring — EIS/AIM lots past their 2-year BPR qualifying date receive stronger drawdown protection than pre-qualifying lots.
F008: BPR cap updated to £2.5M — reflects announced combined BPR/APR allowance. 100% relief up to £2.5M, 50% above. All disclosures and warning thresholds updated.
F009: Net estate after IHT always shown — the amount beneficiaries actually receive, always visible in the estate summary card.

review_request.context: This specification has been through two prior LLM review cycles. v1.1 was reviewed and produced critical fixes (VCT CGT correction, PCLS formula rewrite, tax parameter scheduling, EIS deferred gains, NEFI reclassification downgrade). v1.2 incorporated those fixes. v1.3 adds nine new features from the delivered prototype. We are now requesting a third review cycle before the next development sprint.
review_request.what_we_want_from_reviewers: A rigorous, critical assessment. We do not want validation — we want to find errors before they are built. Previous reviews caught material tax errors (VCT CGT, PCLS formula). We expect this round to catch issues with the new features.
specific_review_questions: 1. WEIGHTED PRIORITY SYSTEM (F001): The engine scores each asset using four weighted dimensions. Is the scoring logic described in 'drawdown_priority_scoring' sound? Are there edge cases where the weighting produces perverse results — e.g., drawing BPR-qualifying EIS before cash at extreme weight combinations?

2. CASH BUFFER (F003): The engine enforces a hard floor across all cash-class assets. What happens in a year where the spend target cannot be met even from non-cash assets, but drawing cash would breach the floor? The spec must define this failure mode explicitly.

3. LEGACY TARGET (F004): The engine caps drawdowns when the portfolio approaches the legacy target. How should the engine handle a year where the spend target and legacy floor are in direct conflict — i.e., meeting the spend target would breach the legacy floor? Which takes precedence and how is this surfaced to the user?

4. NO-PLAN IHT BASELINE (F005): The no-plan baseline grows all assets at their assumed_growth_rate with no draws or gifts. Is this the right baseline? Should it apply BPR relief (since BPR would still apply at death even with no plan)? Should it model the 2026/2027 rule changes in line with the main plan toggles?

5. BPR CAP — RESOLVED IN v1.5: £2.5M per estate (single-individual model, announced December 2025). VCTs confirmed excluded from BPR pool. BPR pool = EIS direct + AIM shares only. Spouse/CP not modelled.

6. BPR-AWARE SCORING (F007): Lots past their 2-year BPR qualifying date receive stronger protection from drawdown under the IHT-reduction weight. Is there a scenario where drawing a qualifying EIS lot is actually IHT-optimal — e.g., when the combined BPR-qualifying total is already well above £2.5M and additional BPR assets provide no marginal IHT benefit?

7. ACTION PLAN ACCURACY (F002): The Action Plan translates simulation outputs into plain-English recommended actions. Are there year-types or draw combinations that would produce misleading plain-English descriptions — e.g., a year where multiple asset classes are drawn simultaneously, or a year where a property disposal produces a large cash surplus carried forward?

8. TAX TREATMENT REVIEW: Has anything changed in UK tax law since the v1.2 review (March 2026) that affects: CGT rates, IHT nil-rate band, pension annual allowance, VCT/EIS limits, or the PCLS/LSA regime? If so, what parameters need updating?

9. SILENT FAILURE RISKS: What data quality issues or edge cases in the mock register or algorithm would cause the engine to produce wrong numbers without any visible error or warning? The previous review identified six silent failures — are there new ones introduced by the F001–F009 features?

10. ACCEPTABLE vs MISLEADING SIMPLIFICATIONS: Given the nine new features, has the balance shifted — are there simplifications that were acceptable in v1.2 that are now misleading given the increased sophistication of the output (e.g., single-individual assumption, no RNRB, flat DB pension)?
review_request.format_requested: For each finding: (1) which section or feature it applies to, (2) severity — Critical (will produce wrong outputs) / Important (logic gap or misleading output) / Minor (edge case or disclosure gap), (3) specific correction or recommendation. Group by severity. Be direct — do not soften findings.

changelog_from_v1.1: CRITICAL FIX: VCT disposal tax corrected — CGT does NOT apply on disposal of qualifying VCT shares regardless of hold period. Early disposal (before 5 years) triggers income tax relief clawback only, not CGT. Previous spec was materially wrong on this point.

CRITICAL FIX: Pension TFLS/PCLS crystallisation formula rewritten. Previous ratio-based formula was mathematically unstable across multiple crystallisation events. New formula: pcls = min(0.25 × gross_crystallisation, remaining_lsa); taxable = gross_crystallisation - pcls; remaining_lsa -= pcls.

CRITICAL FIX: Tax parameter schedule introduced — tax rates, thresholds and allowances must be year-indexed for multi-year plans. Flat 2025/26 parameters cannot be silently applied to year 20 of a plan.
CRITICAL FIX: EIS deferred gains field added to schema — deferred_gain_amount. Deferred gains crystallise on EIS disposal and must be added to taxable income in the disposal year.
CRITICAL FIX: AIM/listed equity CGT rates made explicit — same 18%/24% residential property rates apply to all chargeable assets for individuals in 2025/26. Not 'standard rates' (ambiguous).
CRITICAL FIX: NEFI reclassification downgraded from automatic to advisory warning — HMRC test is looser than the strict income-surplus check. Hard reclassification removed; warning flag retained.

global_assumptions.description: Explicit assumptions that apply to the entire model. These must be disclosed in the UI wherever outputs are shown.
assumptions: We model you as a single UK resident individual. Spouse, civil partner exemptions, transferable nil-rate bands (TNRB), and joint asset transfers are not modelled.
Plan year 1 = the tax year starting 6 April in which the plan is created. All allowances reset on 6 April. Plans do not start mid-tax-year.

The April 2026 BPR cap and April 2027 pension IHT changes are modelled policy assumptions based on proposals announced in October 2024. Neither has been enacted into statute as of March 2026. These scenarios can be toggled on or off.
Defined benefit pensions are treated as a flat annual income stream. Pension capital value, survivor benefits, and commutation rights are not modelled.
State pension is excluded by default. An optional state_pension_annual field can be populated to reduce required drawdown.
All assets are assumed to be UK-sited and owned by a UK resident individual. Non-domiciled status, offshore structures, and corporate ownership are not modelled.
Growth rates are flat annual assumptions. Sequence-of-returns risk and Monte Carlo modelling are deferred to v2.
Residential Nil-Rate Band (RNRB: £175,000 per individual) is not modelled. If your main residence passes to direct descendants, actual IHT may be lower than shown.
Care costs, divorce, and relationship changes are not modelled.

The dividend allowance (£500 for 2025/26) and personal savings allowance (£1,000 basic rate / £500 higher rate / £0 additional rate) are stored as configurable global parameters, not hardcoded constants.

All tax rates, thresholds and allowances are year-indexed via tax_parameter_schedule. Years beyond the schedule use the last known parameters (hold-flat assumption). This must be disclosed in the UI: 'Tax parameters beyond [last scheduled year] are held at [year] values. Actual future rates may differ.'

current_age is required at plan creation to anchor the shadow projection. Shadow horizon = max(plan_years, 35, 90 - current_age). The minimum of 35 ensures the shadow always runs at least 35 years. Example: age 70, plan 20 years → max(20, 35, 20) = 35. Age input range is 55–90 in v1. Disclosed in UI.

Post-2027 pension IHT outputs show estate-level IHT only. Beneficiary-side income tax on inherited pension drawdown is not modelled. The full family tax burden may be higher or lower than shown depending on beneficiary circumstances.

Discretionary trust outputs model entry charges and death interactions only. Ongoing relevant-property trust charges (ten-year periodic charge and exit charges) are not modelled. Users with active discretionary trusts should obtain separate adviser confirmation of ongoing trust costs.

MODELLED ASSUMPTION (effective April 2026, announced December 2025): Combined BPR + APR qualifying assets (EIS direct + AIM shares — VCT excluded) below this threshold: 100% IHT relief. Above: 50% (effective 20% IHT on excess). £2.5M threshold in this single-individual model; spouse/civil partner has a separate threshold — aggregation not modelled.

tax_parameter_schedule.description: Year-indexed tax parameters for multi-year plans. Each entry covers one tax year (6 April to 5 April). The engine must look up parameters by plan_year rather than using flat 2025/26 values. Years beyond the last entry use the final entry's values (hold-flat). The UI must disclose the hold-flat assumption clearly.
tax_parameter_schedule.hold_flat_disclosure: Tax parameters beyond the last scheduled year are held at the most recent known values. Actual future rates, thresholds, and allowances will differ. This planner is not a forecast of future tax law.

schedule.notes: CGT rates unified at 18%/24% from October 2024. VCT income-tax relief 30% — reduces to 20% from 6 April 2026 for new subscriptions.

Dividend rates increased from 2025/26. VCT upfront income-tax relief reduced 30% → 20% from 6 April 2026 for new subscriptions — affects tax_relief_claimed and clawback basis. BPR/APR cap (£2.5M per estate) takes effect from April 2026 under modelled assumption. Savings rates planned to change from 6 April 2027 (22%/42%/47%) — not yet enacted.

tax_parameter_schedule.implementation_note: When a plan crosses a tax year boundary mid-simulation (all plans do, since plan years align to 6 April), apply the parameters for the tax year containing that plan year. For example, plan year 3 starting April 2028 uses 2028/29 parameters if they exist in the schedule, otherwise holds at the last known entry.

product_context.description: Unlock is a UK wealth intelligence platform for high-net-worth private investors with portfolios of £250k–£5M+. It provides institutional-grade portfolio intelligence including an asset register, tax modelling, EIS/VCT deal flow, and — via this module — decumulation planning.
product_context.target_user: UK HNW investor, typically aged 55–75, with complex multi-asset portfolios including ISAs, pensions, VCTs, EIS direct investments, rental property, and cash. Often self-directed or adviser-informed rather than fully advised.
product_context.module_description: A planning tool that answers: given my assets (loaded from the Unlock asset register), how long will my money last if I spend X per year at Y lifestyle level, and what is the optimal sequence to draw assets to minimise tax and maximise the estate passed to heirs?
product_context.key_design_principle: Assets are NEVER manually entered by the user. They flow from the Unlock asset register, which holds lot-level data including acquisition dates, acquisition costs, wrapper types, and tax relief claimed on entry. The planner is a read-and-compute layer on top of the register.
product_context.reference_benchmark: The 2010 Towry Lifetime Financial Plan (a traditional IFA-produced decumulation report). Unlock should produce an equivalent output dynamically, in real time, covering asset classes IFAs cannot advise on (VCTs, EIS direct), and updating continuously as the register changes.

scenario_toggles.description: Future rule changes that are modelled as assumptions, not enacted law. These must appear as toggleable scenarios in the UI with a clear disclosure banner. Default is ON to encourage planning ahead, but users must be told these are not current law.

toggles.label: Apply proposed 2026 BPR/APR cap (£2.5M per estate at 100%, 50% above)
toggles.disclaimer: This scenario is based on proposals announced in the October 2024 Autumn Budget. It has not been enacted into law as of March 2026. Actual rules may differ.

toggles.label: Apply proposed 2027 pension IHT inclusion
toggles.disclaimer: This scenario is based on proposals announced in the October 2024 Autumn Budget. It has not been enacted into law as of March 2026. Actual rules may differ.

user_inputs.description: The only three inputs the user provides. Everything else is computed from the register or offered as optional configuration.

inputs.label: Annual income needed
inputs.description: The base annual spending target in today's money, before lifestyle multiplier and before inflation adjustment

inputs.label: Plan for how many years
inputs.description: The number of years the plan should cover. Longevity risk is the primary failure mode — users should be encouraged to plan to at least age 90 (typically 25–35 years). Algorithm always also runs a 35-year shadow projection regardless of this input.

inputs.label: Lifestyle level
inputs.description: Applied as a multiplier to annual_income_target to produce the real annual spend target

derived_inputs.description: Applied annually to compound the spending target in nominal terms

DEPRECATED from v1.3. The four strategies are now preset buttons that populate priority_weights sliders. Retained in algorithm.drawdown_strategies as reference only and for benchmark comparison UI columns. Do not expose as a user-facing selector in v1.5+.
Annual amount transferred to trust or gifted to family. Removed from estate each year before drawdown calculation.
Annual state pension income in today's money. Reduces required drawdown from assets each year. Default 0 — omitting it overstates asset consumption slightly but is the conservative assumption.
User's current age at plan creation. Required. Range 55–90 in v1. Shadow horizon = max(plan_years, 35, 90 - current_age).

asset_register_schema.description: The data structure Unlock's asset register must supply per asset holding. Lot-level granularity is required for VCTs and EIS — a single holding may have multiple lots with different acquisition dates and therefore different BPR/CGT clocks. wrapper_type and asset_class are separate fields — the combination determines tax treatment.
asset_register_schema.tax_treatment_derived_from: wrapper_type + asset_class combination. Example: aim_shares in wrapper_type=isa are taxed as ISA (growth/income tax-free, IHT in estate) not as unwrapped AIM (dividends taxable, CGT on disposal, BPR after 2 years).

required_fields_per_lot.description: Unique identifier per lot
isa / pension / unwrapped / trust / company. NEW in v1.1. Combined with asset_class to derive tax treatment.
Underlying asset type — see asset_classes above. Separate from wrapper_type.
Human-readable name e.g. 'Octopus Titan VCT — 2021 tranche'
Current market or estimated value in GBP

Date of purchase/subscription. Required for: CGT clock (all asset classes), BPR clock (EIS/AIM only — NOT VCT), VCT clawback clock (5yr), VCT CGT-free clock (5yr). If missing for EIS/AIM: flag and exclude from BPR calculation. If missing for VCT: flag and exclude from CGT-free and clawback calculations.
Amount paid at acquisition before any tax relief. Critical for CGT base cost. If missing for any asset where CGT applies on draw, flag and exclude from CGT calculation — never default to zero.
Income tax relief received on entry (e.g. 30% EIS/VCT). Affects net cost basis for loss relief calculations.
Annual growth rate assumption for this asset. Editable per lot or set by asset class default.
Annual income produced by this asset (rent, dividends, interest). Used for income tax stacking calculation.

Applies ONLY to EIS direct investments and AIM shares. Never to VCT — VCTs are listed securities that do not qualify for Business Property Relief (IHTA 1984 s.105(3), HMRC IHTM18113). For EIS/AIM: set based on adviser confirmation. Never assume true if acquisition_date is missing. For VCT lots this field must be false — warning VCT_IHT_EXEMPT_FLAG_INCORRECT fires if true on a VCT lot.
Computed from acquisition_date + 2 years. Applies ONLY to EIS direct and AIM shares. Null for VCT (no BPR qualification). Null if acquisition_date is missing.
Date BPR qualification was last confirmed. EIS/AIM only — not applicable to VCT. Flag BPR_LAST_REVIEWED_STALE if > 12 months old for EIS/AIM lots.
Date on which disposal becomes CGT-free. VCT: acquisition_date + 5 years. EIS: acquisition_date + 3 years. Null if acquisition_date missing.
For property: outstanding mortgage. Net equity = current_value - mortgage_balance.
For pensions: defined_contribution / defined_benefit / sipp / workplace. Affects draw rules.

NEW in v1.1 — REPLACES tfls_taken boolean. Cumulative TFLS drawn from this individual's pensions across all schemes in GBP. Remaining allowance = global_parameters.tfls_lifetime_limit - tfls_used_amount. If 0: TFLS not yet used.
NEW in v1.1. True if flexi-access drawdown has been taken and MPAA is in force. More precise than in_drawdown proxy. When true, future pension contributions capped at mpaa (£10,000/year).
True if pension is already in flexible drawdown phase. Used for display/status only in v1.1 — MPAA logic uses mpaa_triggered.
NEW in v1.1. True if ISA wrapper permits withdrawn funds to be redeposited in the same tax year without consuming additional annual allowance.

For EIS lots where CGT deferral relief was used on entry: the gain originally deferred. On disposal, this gain is REVIVED and subject to CGT — NOT added to income tax as non-savings income. Stacked on top of taxable income to determine CGT rate (18%/24%) but does not enter the income tax band calculation. If null: flag EIS_DEFERRED_GAIN_MISSING — disposal-year CGT may be understated.

NEW in v1.2. For VCT/EIS lots: type of relief claimed on entry — 'income_tax_relief' / 'cgt_deferral' / 'both' / 'none'. Drives clawback and disposal-year tax calculations. VCT lots with relief_claimed_type=none have no clawback on early disposal.

NEW in v1.2. For property: cumulative allowable capital improvement costs since acquisition. Added to acquisition_cost when calculating CGT gain. Default 0. Do not include repair/maintenance (revenue costs).

NEW in v1.2. For property: estimated disposal costs as a percentage of sale proceeds (agent fees, legal costs, etc.). Default 2.5%. Deducted from disposal proceeds before CGT calculation. Alternative to estimated_disposal_cost_amount — use one or the other.
NEW in v1.2. For property: fixed estimated disposal cost in GBP. Takes precedence over estimated_disposal_cost_pct if both present.

lot_disposal_methodology.description: UK share matching rules (TCGA 1992 s.104) govern which acquisition lots are matched against disposals for CGT purposes. Full HMRC share matching is not implemented in v1. A disclosed average-cost proxy is used instead.
lot_disposal_methodology.v1_proxy: Average cost basis: total acquisition_cost across all lots of same asset / total units. CGT gain = disposal_proceeds - (average_cost_per_unit × units_disposed). This is a planning approximation only — actual CGT may differ if lot-level matching produces different results.
lot_disposal_methodology.disclosure: This planner uses an average-cost approximation for CGT on multi-lot holdings. Actual CGT liability depends on HMRC share matching rules and may differ. For disposal decisions, users should obtain specific tax advice.
lot_disposal_methodology.exception: VCT and EIS lots must ALWAYS be treated at individual lot level — never averaged. Each lot has its own relief_claimed_type, revived_deferred_gain, acquisition_date, and clawback exposure. Averaging across VCT/EIS lots would produce incorrect clawback and revived-deferred-gain calculations.

vct_relief_rate_by_subscription_date.description: VCT upfront income-tax relief rate is determined by subscription date.
rules: Pre 6 April 2026: relief rate = 30%. Clawback on early disposal = 30% × subscription_amount.
On or after 6 April 2026: relief rate = 20%. Clawback = 20% × subscription_amount.
Rate is locked at subscription date — not at disposal date or current tax year.
Engine reads tax_relief_claimed from lot record. Never re-derive from a fixed global rate.
vct_relief_rate_by_subscription_date.onboarding_validation: Warn VCT_RELIEF_RATE_MISMATCH if tax_relief_claimed / subscription_amount is not ~0.30 for pre-April-2026 lots or ~0.20 for post-April-2026 lots.

asset_tax_matrix.description: Tax treatment per asset class. Tax is derived from wrapper_type + asset_class. The treatments below assume wrapper_type=unwrapped unless stated. For assets held inside ISA or pension wrappers, the wrapper tax rules override the underlying asset rules.

wrapper_override_rules.isa: All growth and income tax-free within wrapper. Withdrawal tax-free. IHT: in estate at full value (no exemption). Annual contribution limit: £20,000. Withdrawn funds cannot be redeposited unless flexible_isa=true.
wrapper_override_rules.pension: All growth tax-free within wrapper. Contributions receive income tax relief at marginal rate. Withdrawal: 25% tax-free (subject to tfls_used_amount vs tfls_lifetime_limit), remainder taxed as income at marginal rate. IHT: see pension entry in assets array.

assets.while_held: Interest taxed as income above personal savings allowance (PSA). PSA is configurable: £1,000 basic rate, £500 higher rate, £0 additional rate.
assets.on_draw: No tax event on withdrawal. Interest taxed annually as it arises.
assets.iht: In estate at full value. No exemption.
assets.drawdown_priority_notes: Draw first. No tax on withdrawal, lowest growth rate, erodes in real terms.

assets.while_held: All growth and income tax-free within wrapper.
assets.on_draw: Completely tax-free. No income tax, no CGT.
assets.iht: In estate at full value. No exemption.
assets.drawdown_priority_notes: Draw after cash. Tax-free withdrawal but once drawn, contribution allowance for that tax year is permanently lost (unless flexible_isa=true). Do not over-draw from ISA.

limits.note: Cannot be refilled after withdrawal unless flexible_isa=true

assets._note: ISA is a wrapper type, not an asset class. This entry documents wrapper_type=isa behaviour only. The canonical definition is in asset_tax_matrix.wrapper_override_rules.isa. ISA does not appear as asset_class in the register schema.

assets.on_entry: Tax relief at marginal rate on contributions. Annual allowance £60,000 or 100% earnings (lower prevails). Carry-forward of unused allowance from 3 prior tax years permitted.

on_draw.tax_free_lump_sum: Up to 25% of each crystallisation event is tax-free as a Pension Commencement Lump Sum (PCLS), subject to the individual's remaining Lump Sum Allowance (LSA). LSA remaining = tfls_lifetime_limit (£268,275) - tfls_used_amount.
on_draw.tfls_crystallisation_formula: CORRECTED in v1.2. For each pension draw event: (1) gross_crystallisation = total pension amount being crystallised to fund this year's draw. (2) max_pcls_this_event = 0.25 × gross_crystallisation. (3) pcls = min(max_pcls_this_event, remaining_lsa). (4) taxable_pension_draw = gross_crystallisation - pcls. (5) remaining_lsa -= pcls. (6) Update tfls_used_amount += pcls. This formula is stable across multiple crystallisation events and matches HMRC's Lump Sum Allowance mechanics. The previous ratio-based formula in v1.1 was mathematically unstable when partial crystallisations stacked across years.
on_draw.tfls_exhaustion: Once remaining_lsa = 0, pcls = 0 for all future events. All further pension draws are 100% taxable income at marginal rate. The algorithm must track remaining_lsa as a running balance across plan years.
on_draw.remainder: All pension draws above pcls are taxable income at marginal rate. Stack on top of all other income per income_type_ordering in algorithm step 3.
on_draw.mpaa: If mpaa_triggered=true, future pension contributions are capped at £10,000/year (MPAA). Triggered by taking flexi-access drawdown or UFPLS — not by PCLS alone or some annuity purchases.

iht.current_law: Undrawn pension pots are currently outside the estate for IHT purposes — a key reason to draw pension last and preserve for heirs.
iht.modelled_assumption_april_2027: MODELLED ASSUMPTION (not enacted law): If scenario_toggle apply_2027_pension_iht=true, from April 2027 undrawn pension pots are included in the estate and subject to IHT at 40%. This inverts the draw-last logic. The algorithm must flag the April 2027 year in outputs and model both pre- and post-April-2027 IHT positions.
iht.planning_implication: If apply_2027_pension_iht=true, users with large pension pots should consider accelerating drawdown before April 2027, accepting income tax now to reduce future IHT. Algorithm should surface this comparison.

assets.drawdown_priority_notes: Complex. Pre-April 2027 (or if toggle off): draw last — IHT-exempt. Post-April 2027 (if toggle on): model whether accelerating draws before April 2027 saves total tax. Must calculate marginal income tax rate each year before drawing.

limits.note: Tracked via tfls_used_amount — not a per-year limit

assets.on_entry: No income tax relief on purchase. SDLT applies at acquisition (not modelled in drawdown phase).
assets.while_held: Rental income taxed as income at marginal rate. Mortgage interest relief restricted to 20% tax credit for individuals (Section 24). Maintenance, letting agent fees, and other allowable expenses deductible.
assets.on_draw: Disposal triggers CGT. Gain = disposal proceeds - acquisition_cost - allowable improvement costs. CGT rate: 18% if total income (including gain) in disposal year falls within basic rate band; 24% if higher/additional rate. Annual CGT exempt amount: £3,000 (configurable). Property is indivisible — disposal is a full binary event, not a partial draw.
assets.disposal_proceeds_rule: Net disposal proceeds (after CGT and costs) are transferred to cash in the year of disposal and used as a funding source in that year and subsequent years. Any net proceeds exceeding that year's spend target remain in cash for future years. Implementers must not discard or ignore proceeds that exceed the current-year target.
assets.iht: In estate at full value. No BPR exemption.
assets.cgt: 18% or 24% depending on income in disposal year. £3,000 annual exempt amount. Full disposal event — model as single lump in disposal year.
assets.drawdown_priority_notes: Illiquid. Plan disposal year to minimise CGT rate — avoid years with high pension income. Consider sequencing property sales in years with low other income.

limits.note: Full property sale only — cannot partially dispose

assets.on_entry: 30% income tax relief on investment in new shares (not secondary market). Annual subscription limit £200,000 including reinvested dividends. Reinvested dividends that are issued as new shares count toward the £200k limit — register must distinguish new share issuance from simple accumulation.
assets.while_held: Cash and reinvested dividends tax-free regardless of amount.

on_draw.after_5_years: No CGT on disposal. Tax-free. Income tax relief not clawed back. Full disposal proceeds retained.
on_draw.before_5_years_CORRECTED: CRITICAL CORRECTION from v1.1: CGT does NOT apply on disposal of qualifying VCT shares regardless of hold period. VCT shares are explicitly exempt from CGT on disposal under TCGA 1992 s.151A. The consequence of early disposal (before 5 years, or before 3 years for shares acquired before April 2004) is NOT CGT — it is clawback of the upfront income tax relief at 30%. The relief is clawed back in full if disposed within the qualifying holding period. There is no partial or tapered clawback — it is all-or-nothing based on whether the qualifying period was met.
on_draw.early_disposal_mechanics: If disposed before 5-year qualifying period: (1) Income tax relief clawback = 30% × original subscription amount for that lot. (2) CGT = £0 — no CGT on VCT disposal regardless of gain. (3) Net return = disposal_proceeds - relief_clawback. Note: if no income tax relief was originally claimed on a lot (e.g. bought on secondary market), there is no clawback even on early disposal.
on_draw.lot_level_requirement: Clawback is calculated per lot using the original subscription amount and whether relief was claimed. tax_relief_claimed field on the lot is the clawback base. Lots where tax_relief_claimed = 0 have no clawback exposure.

assets.iht: IN ESTATE at full value. VCTs are listed securities and do NOT qualify for Business Property Relief (IHTA 1984 s.105(3), HMRC IHTM18113). Never included in the £2.5M BPR/APR cap pool. is_iht_exempt must always be false for VCT lots. No BPR qualifying period applies. Any historic claim that VCTs are 'commonly BPR-qualifying' is incorrect for IHT planning purposes.
assets.cgt: CGT-free after 5-year hold from acquisition_date. CGT at standard rates before 5 years.
assets.drawdown_priority_notes: Draw after non-BPR in-estate assets (cash, ISA, property) but before EIS — VCT disposals are CGT-exempt (post 5yr), EIS are BPR-qualifying. VCTs are in-estate for IHT with no exemption. Each lot's 5yr CGT-free and clawback clocks are lot-level — acquisition_date essential.

limits.note: Includes reinvested dividends issued as new shares
CGT applies on disposal before 5 years
30% relief clawed back if disposed before 5 years

assets.on_entry: 30% income tax relief on investment. Annual limit £1,000,000 (£2,000,000 for knowledge-intensive companies). CGT deferral available on gains reinvested into EIS — deferred CGT crystallises on EIS disposal.
assets.while_held: Growth tax-free within EIS wrapper. Dividends uncommon — EIS companies typically growth-focused.

on_draw.after_3_years_success: CGT-free on disposal provided: (1) 3-year hold condition met from acquisition_date, (2) income tax relief was not withdrawn.
on_draw.loss: If company fails: loss relief available against income tax at marginal rate, net of income tax relief already received on entry. Effective floor on downside.
on_draw.cgt_deferral_crystallisation_CORRECTED: EIS revived deferred gains (revived_deferred_gain) are CGT events, NOT income tax events. They crystallise on disposal as a revived deferred gain and are charged to CGT at the applicable rate (18%/24% depending on total income in the disposal year). They do NOT enter the income tax calculation as non-savings income. However, they DO affect the CGT rate calculation: the revived gain is stacked on top of taxable income when determining whether the basic-rate or higher-rate CGT band applies. Disposal-year tax: CGT += max(0, revived_deferred_gain - cgt_exempt_amount_remaining) × cgt_rate.

iht.current_law: 100% BPR exemption after 2-year hold where company remains a qualifying trading company.
iht.modelled_assumption_april_2026: MODELLED ASSUMPTION (not enacted law, announced December 2025 effective April 2026): If apply_2026_bpr_cap=true, IHT relief on combined BPR/APR-qualifying assets (EIS + AIM shares only — VCT excluded) is capped at £2,500,000 per estate. First £2.5M at 100% relief. Above £2.5M at 50% relief (effective 20% IHT on the excess). Applied per estate — spouses/civil partners each have their own £2.5M allowance. Order of asset assessment: AIM first, then EIS (oldest acquisition date first within class) — algorithm must define and document ordering consistently.

assets.cgt: CGT-free after 3-year hold if relief intact. CGT deferral from entry crystallises on disposal. Standard rates before 3 years.
assets.drawdown_priority_notes: Draw last. Highest growth rate assumption, BPR-qualifying (100% up to £2.5M per estate combined with AIM). Loss relief limits downside. Note: VCTs are NOT in the BPR pool — this cap is EIS + AIM only.

limits.note: £2.5M threshold in this single-individual model; spouse/civil partner has a separate threshold — aggregation not modelled. EIS direct + AIM shares only — VCT excluded.

assets.on_entry: No income tax relief on purchase.
assets.while_held: Dividends taxed above dividend allowance (£500 for 2025/26, configurable) at dividend rates. Growth not taxed until disposal.
assets.on_draw: Disposal triggers CGT. Gain = disposal_proceeds - acquisition_cost - cgt_exempt_amount. CGT rates for 2025/26: 18% where total income plus gain falls within basic rate band; 24% where total income plus gain exceeds basic rate threshold. These are the same rates as residential property following the October 2024 Budget unification. Lot disposal follows the v1 lot_disposal_methodology (average-cost proxy — see schema). Annual CGT exempt amount: £3,000 (year-indexed via tax_parameter_schedule).

iht.current_law: BPR-qualifying after 2-year hold where company remains qualifying trading. 100% IHT exemption.
iht.modelled_assumption_april_2026: MODELLED ASSUMPTION (announced December 2025, effective April 2026): If apply_2026_bpr_cap=true, AIM shares are included in the combined £2.5M BPR/APR cap per estate alongside EIS direct investments. VCTs are NOT included — they are listed and do not qualify for Business Relief.

assets.cgt: Standard CGT rates. Annual exempt amount £3,000.
assets.drawdown_priority_notes: Draw before VCTs and EIS but after non-BPR assets. BPR-qualifying but no income tax relief on entry makes them less tax-advantaged overall.

limits.note: £2.5M threshold in this single-individual model; spouse/civil partner has a separate threshold — aggregation not modelled. EIS direct + AIM shares only — VCT excluded.

algorithm.description: The decumulation simulation runs year-by-year from year 1 to plan_years. Each year is a constrained annual optimisation: solve for the combination of asset draws that meets the income target while respecting all tax limits, wrapper rules, and BPR clocks. This is a myopic (year-by-year greedy) optimiser — it does not globally minimise tax across the full plan horizon. Multi-year global optimisation is deferred to v2.

annual_computation_sequence.name: Grow all assets
annual_computation_sequence.description: Apply each asset's assumed_growth_rate. For property: growth applies to gross value, not net equity. For VCTs: reinvested dividends issued as new shares are added to value and count toward the annual £200k subscription limit. State pension (if state_pension_annual > 0) is treated as baseline income — it does not 'grow' but inflation-adjusts at the same rate as the spend target.

annual_computation_sequence.name: Calculate baseline income
annual_computation_sequence.description: Sum all income generated by assets that does not require a draw decision: rental income, interest, dividends, state_pension_annual (inflation-adjusted). Track three parallel fields — these are not the same and must not be conflated:

income_tracks.cash_income: Total cash received from income-generating assets. Used for cashflow: does this year's income + draws cover spend target?
income_tracks.taxable_income_pre_allowances: Gross taxable income by type before applying personal allowance, PSA, or dividend allowance. Used as input to band calculation in step 3.
income_tracks.taxable_income_post_allowances: Taxable income after subtracting personal allowance, PSA (for savings income), and dividend allowance (for dividend income). This is the figure that actually consumes tax bands and drives marginal rate on pension draws.

annual_computation_sequence.example: A user receives £5,000 bank interest. cash_income += £5,000. taxable_income_pre_allowances (savings) += £5,000. If basic rate taxpayer: PSA = £1,000, so taxable_income_post_allowances (savings) += £4,000 at 20%. The remaining £1,000 is cash received but not taxable — it does NOT consume the basic rate band.
annual_computation_sequence.state_pension_treatment: If state_pension_annual > 0: apply inflation uplift at same rate as spend target. Add to cash_income and to taxable_income_pre_allowances as non-savings income. It counts against the personal allowance in full.

annual_computation_sequence.name: Calculate available tax bands
annual_computation_sequence.description: Using taxable_income_post_allowances from step 2, determine remaining headroom in each band. Look up current year's parameters from tax_parameter_schedule by plan_year. Do not use flat 2025/26 constants.
annual_computation_sequence.income_type_ordering: UK income tax stacks income types in this order for band consumption: (1) non-savings income first (pension draws, rental income, employment income, state pension), (2) savings income second (interest net of PSA), (3) dividend income last (net of dividend allowance). This ordering determines how much basic-rate band remains for pension draws after rental income has consumed part of it.
annual_computation_sequence.personal_allowance_taper_formula: If adjusted_net_income > pa_taper_start: personal_allowance = max(0, personal_allowance - floor((adjusted_net_income - pa_taper_start) / 2)). Reaches zero at higher_rate_threshold. All values from tax_parameter_schedule[plan_year].
annual_computation_sequence.tax_reserve_note: Tax due on baseline income (rental tax, interest tax, dividend tax) must be funded from cash. If baseline_cash_income is insufficient to cover both spending and this tax, the shortfall must be sourced from asset draws — it is real cash that must leave the portfolio. Step 6 must account for this.

annual_computation_sequence.name: Process gifts and trust transfers
annual_computation_sequence.description: Remove annual_gift_amount from estate. Apply gift exemptions and classify in this order: (1) Apply annual gift exemption (£3,000, carry forward one year max £6,000) first — this portion is immediately IHT-free and does NOT count toward CLT rolling total. (2) Remaining gift: classify as CLT (discretionary trust) or PET (bare trust/direct gift) per user's selected gift type. (3) For CLTs: check rolling 7-year cumulative total — warn if approaching £325,000 NRB. (4) For PETs: record gift date and amount for taper relief calculation if death occurs within 7 years. Draw gift from cash first, then ISA. Check NEFI conditions if gift exceeds baseline income surplus.
annual_computation_sequence.annual_exemption_ordering: CRITICAL: The £3,000 annual exemption (and £3,000 carry-forward if available) must be applied and deducted BEFORE classifying any gift as a CLT or PET. It never counts toward the CLT 7-year rolling total.

annual_computation_sequence.name: Calculate inflation-adjusted spend target
annual_computation_sequence.description: spend_target_year_N = annual_income_target × lifestyle_multiplier × (1 + inflation_rate)^(N-1). This is in nominal GBP. Shortfall is calculated against this nominal figure.
annual_computation_sequence.net_required_draw: net_required_draw_year_N = spend_target_year_N + tax_reserve_on_baseline_income - baseline_cash_income_year_N. The tax reserve is added because tax on rental/interest income is a real cash outflow that must be funded. If net_required_draw <= 0: no asset draw required this year. Record surplus cash.

annual_computation_sequence.name: Draw assets in priority order
annual_computation_sequence.description: For each asset in the drawdown strategy's priority queue, in order: (a) calculate maximum draw available without triggering an avoidable tax event, (b) calculate tax cost of drawing from this asset at current marginal rate, (c) draw up to what is needed to meet net_required_draw_year_N. Continue down priority queue until target is met or all assets exhausted.
annual_computation_sequence.pension_draw_logic: Before drawing pension: check remaining_basic_rate_band from step 3. Draw only up to the amount that keeps total income within the current tax band unless the spend target cannot otherwise be met. Apply corrected PCLS formula: (1) gross_crystallisation = amount to draw from pension. (2) max_pcls_this_event = 0.25 × gross_crystallisation. (3) pcls = min(max_pcls_this_event, remaining_lsa). (4) taxable_pension_draw = gross_crystallisation - pcls. (5) Update remaining_lsa and tfls_used_amount. Check that adding taxable_pension_draw to existing taxable income does not push into a higher band unnecessarily — if it does, reduce gross_crystallisation and source the shortfall from the next asset in priority order.
annual_computation_sequence.property_draw_logic: Property is not a regular annual draw. Model property disposal as a discrete event in a specified year (either user-selected or algorithm-recommended as a low-income year). In the disposal year: calculate gain = disposal_proceeds - acquisition_cost - allowable_costs. Calculate CGT = max(0, gain - cgt_exempt_amount) × cgt_rate (where cgt_rate depends on total income including gain in that year). Net proceeds = disposal_proceeds - CGT - disposal_costs. Transfer net_proceeds to cash. In the disposal year, use proceeds to meet spend target first, then carry remainder forward in cash for future years.
annual_computation_sequence.tax_drag_calculation: For each draw amount by asset type: income_tax = amount × marginal_rate (for pension draws). CGT = (disposal_proceeds - acquisition_cost - allowable_costs - cgt_exempt_amount) × cgt_rate (for property/AIM/EIS pre-clock). Net draw = gross draw - tax_paid.

annual_computation_sequence.name: Check BPR clocks and IHT position
annual_computation_sequence.description: For each EIS direct and AIM lot (NOT VCT — VCTs have no BPR clock): verify acquisition_date + 2 years has passed and is_iht_exempt=true. If not yet qualifying, flag BPR_NOT_YET_QUALIFYING. Calculate estimated_iht_bill as follows.

iht_bill_calculation.step_a: Sum all BPR-qualifying asset values where is_iht_exempt=true and bpr_qualifying_date has passed. Call this bpr_total.
iht_bill_calculation.step_b_apply_bpr_cap: If apply_2026_bpr_cap=true: bpr_exempt = min(bpr_total, bpr_full_relief_cap) + (max(0, bpr_total - bpr_full_relief_cap) × 0.5). Effective relief = bpr_exempt. If apply_2026_bpr_cap=false: bpr_exempt = bpr_total.
iht_bill_calculation.step_c_net_taxable_estate: taxable_estate = total_portfolio_value - bpr_exempt. If apply_2027_pension_iht=false: subtract undrawn pension value from taxable_estate (pension outside estate). If apply_2027_pension_iht=true and plan year > April 2027: include pension in taxable_estate.
iht_bill_calculation.step_d_nrb_stacking: CRITICAL FIX from v1.0: Apply nil-rate band correctly accounting for prior chargeable transfers. (1) Sum all CLTs made in the last 7 years (clt_7yr_cumulative). (2) Available NRB = max(0, nil_rate_band - clt_7yr_cumulative). (3) PETs that become chargeable (if death within 7 years) also eat into NRB — but for the annual snapshot, use CLT total only. (4) iht_on_estate = max(0, taxable_estate - available_nrb) × iht_rate.
iht_bill_calculation.step_e: estimated_iht_bill = iht_on_estate (for annual snapshot). At plan end (notional death), also add any PETs within 7 years that would crystallise as chargeable, applying taper relief schedule.

annual_computation_sequence.name: Check scenario trigger years
annual_computation_sequence.description: If apply_2027_pension_iht=true and plan_year crosses April 2027: add flag 'PENSION_IHT_RULE_CHANGE_YEAR' to output flags. Recalculate estimated_iht_bill for that year and all future years including pension in taxable estate. If apply_2026_bpr_cap=true and plan_year crosses April 2026: add flag 'BPR_CAP_RULE_CHANGE_YEAR'. Apply tiered BPR relief from that year forward.

annual_computation_sequence.name: Record year snapshot
annual_computation_sequence.description: Store all output fields per the output_schema. Evaluate all warning_triggers and populate flags array.
annual_computation_sequence.shortfall_behaviour: If net_required_draw cannot be met even after drawing all liquid assets: record shortfall = spend_target_year_N - actual_income_year_N. Do not draw from property in shortfall years unless the user has pre-selected a disposal year. Flag shortfall prominently in UI.

tax_optimised.description: Minimise income tax and CGT drag over the plan period
tax_optimised.logic: Draw cash first (no tax event). Draw ISA (tax-free). Draw pension up to band threshold. Property disposal planned in a low-income year. VCT drawn after ISA — CGT-exempt on disposal but in-estate for IHT. EIS last — CGT-exempt after 3yr and BPR-qualifying.

iht_optimised.description: Maximise the estate value passed to heirs
iht_optimised.logic: Draw pension early (especially pre-2027). Draw property before appreciation. Draw VCT before EIS/AIM — VCTs are in-estate with no BPR, so drawing them reduces taxable estate. Preserve EIS and AIM shares for BPR. Preserve ISA as non-BPR estate value for liquidity.

income_first.description: Draw income-producing assets first, let growth assets compound

growth_first.description: Draw slow-growth assets first, let high-growth assets compound

property_valuation_layer.description: Three distinct value fields must be maintained for property throughout.

fields.display_value_gross: Gross market value. Used in portfolio charts and total_portfolio_value display.
fields.funding_value_net: Net cashflow on disposal: gross_proceeds - mortgage_balance - disposal_costs - CGT. Used in step 6 cashflow.
fields.estate_value_iht: Gross value minus secured deductible liabilities (mortgage) per IHTA 1984 s.162. Used in step 7 IHT calculation. Disposal costs not deducted for IHT.

algorithm_step_mapping.step_6_disposal: Use funding_value_net for cashflow and spend calculations

trust_and_gifting.description: Modelling of wealth transfer strategies to reduce the taxable estate. All rules are UK HMRC current law for 2025/26 unless marked MODELLED ASSUMPTION.

methods.label: Discretionary Trust — Chargeable Lifetime Transfer (CLT)
methods.limit_description: NRB: £325,000 per rolling 7-year window per individual. Gifts above this trigger immediate IHT at 20% (half the death rate). At death, cumulative CLTs in prior 7 years reduce available NRB before 40% applies to estate.
methods.algorithm_requirement: Track clt_7yr_cumulative as a rolling time-series (not annual total). Gifts made more than 7 years ago fall out of scope and free up NRB capacity. Warn when approaching £325,000. Suggest switching to PET/bare trust when limit approached. Annual gift exemption applied first — only excess counts toward CLT total.
methods.setup_requirement: Requires solicitor. Ongoing trustee administration costs.

methods.label: Bare Trust or Direct Gift — Potentially Exempt Transfer (PET)
methods.limit_description: No limit. Fully exempt if donor survives 7 years. Taper relief applies for years 3–7.

taper_relief_schedule.note: Full 40% IHT if death in this window

methods.algorithm_requirement: Record date and amount of each PET. In IHT calculation at plan end (notional death year): for each PET within 7 years, calculate IHT using taper_relief_schedule based on years between gift date and notional death date. PETs that become chargeable eat into available NRB in the order made (oldest first).

methods.label: Normal Expenditure from Income (NEFI)
methods.limit_description: Unlimited, immediately exempt. HMRC conditions: (1) regular pattern of giving — not a one-off, (2) made from surplus income, not capital, (3) does not affect donor's standard of living.
methods.hmrc_test_nuance: HMRC's own guidance (IHTM14250) is less rigid than a strict income-surplus test. The donor may in some circumstances use capital for living costs and still satisfy the exemption if income was overall sufficient. HMRC considers the pattern of giving, not just a single year's arithmetic.
methods.algorithm_requirement: Apply a conservative eligibility check: if baseline_cash_income_year_N < (spend_target_year_N + annual_gift_amount), raise warning NEFI_CONDITIONS_MAY_NOT_BE_MET. Do NOT automatically reclassify as PET — this is an advisory warning. The user or their adviser must determine whether NEFI conditions are met. The algorithm should surface the concern clearly but leave the classification decision to the user. Recommend user maintains documented evidence of regularity — HMRC form IHT403 should be completed contemporaneously each year.

methods.label: Annual Gift Exemption
methods.limit_description: £3,000 per year per individual, immediately IHT-free. One unused prior year can be carried forward (maximum £6,000 in a single year). Completely separate from PET/CLT rules — does not count toward NRB.
methods.algorithm_requirement: Track unused annual exemption from prior year. Apply before classifying remaining gift as CLT or PET. If annual_gift_amount <= available_annual_exemption (including carry-forward): entire gift is immediately exempt, zero CLT/PET. If annual_gift_amount > available_annual_exemption: exempt portion = available_annual_exemption; remainder classified as CLT or PET.

hard_constraints_and_failure_modes.description: The limits that cause incorrect or misleading results if not enforced. Primary risk areas for v1 implementation.

constraints.description: TFLS is not 25% of every annual draw. It is a lifetime allowance of £268,275 tracked via tfls_used_amount. Each crystallisation event draws down the remaining allowance. Once exhausted, all future pension draws are 100% taxable income.
constraints.failure_mode: If modelled as 25% tax-free on every draw, income tax liability is materially understated across all plan years.

constraints.description: NEW in v1.1. Users with multiple pension schemes may have taken TFLS from one scheme already. tfls_used_amount captures cumulative TFLS taken across ALL schemes. If this is incorrectly set to 0 at import for a user with prior TFLS history, the model will offer tax-free headroom that does not exist.
constraints.failure_mode: Silent income tax understatement. No error — just wrong numbers. Data import must capture TFLS history accurately.

constraints.description: NEW FIX in v1.1. The IHT estimated_iht_bill calculation must account for CLTs made in the last 7 years when applying the nil-rate band. CLTs reduce available NRB before 40% is applied to the estate. The v1.0 formula (total - iht_exempt - full_NRB) × 0.4 was incorrect when clt_7yr_cumulative > 0.
constraints.failure_mode: IHT bill understated for users with active discretionary trusts. Silent failure — no error, just wrong numbers.

constraints.description: Once flexi-access drawdown or UFPLS is taken, MPAA drops to £10,000. Tracked via mpaa_triggered (not in_drawdown). Affects users still contributing to pensions during drawdown.
constraints.failure_mode: If MPAA trigger ignored, future contribution capacity is overstated.

constraints.description: MODELLED ASSUMPTION. If apply_2027_pension_iht=true, from April 2027 undrawn pension pots are included in the taxable estate. This inverts the draw-last logic for IHT optimisation. Algorithm must flag the April 2027 year and model both regimes.
constraints.failure_mode: Plans that assume pension remains IHT-exempt post-April 2027 will materially understate future IHT bills for users with large pension pots.

constraints.description: MODELLED ASSUMPTION (announced December 2025, effective April 2026). If apply_2026_bpr_cap=true, combined BPR/APR-qualifying assets (EIS direct + AIM shares only — VCT excluded as listed securities) are tiered: 100% relief up to £2.5M per estate, 50% above. Algorithm must track combined EIS+AIM total. VCTs are never included in this pool.
constraints.failure_mode: Users with >£2.5M in combined EIS+AIM assets will have materially understated IHT bills if the cap is not modelled. Also: incorrectly including VCT in the BPR pool overstates IHT shelter.

constraints.description: NEW in v1.1. AIM shares or EIS held inside an ISA wrapper must be taxed under ISA rules (growth/income tax-free, no CGT, IHT in estate) — NOT under AIM/EIS rules. wrapper_type must override asset_class for tax treatment. If this is not implemented, AIM-in-ISA will be incorrectly taxed.
constraints.failure_mode: CGT applied to ISA-wrapped assets. BPR exemption incorrectly claimed for ISA-wrapped AIM shares.

constraints.description: VCT subscriptions including reinvested dividends issued as new shares are capped at £200,000 per tax year. Reinvested dividends that push total over £200k trigger clawback of income tax relief on the excess. Register must distinguish new share issuance from simple accumulation.
constraints.failure_mode: Reinvested dividend compounding may exceed limit without user awareness.

constraints.description: ISA withdrawals permanently consume that year's subscription allowance unless flexible_isa=true. Drawn funds cannot simply be re-deposited.
constraints.failure_mode: If modelled as freely refillable, future ISA compounding is overstated.

constraints.description: Investment property cannot be partially disposed of. Model as a discrete full-disposal event in a specified year. Net proceeds transfer to cash and fund subsequent years.
constraints.failure_mode: Treating property as a liquid annual drawdown source produces meaningless numbers.

constraints.description: Pension drawdown, rental income, interest, and employment income stack for income tax. Marginal rate on pension draws depends on total income. Algorithm must calculate blended rate for each draw decision using income_type_ordering from step 3.
constraints.failure_mode: User with £40k rental income who draws £30k pension pays higher-rate tax on pension draw. Modelling as basic rate understates income tax by approximately £4,000/year.

constraints.description: Property CGT rate depends on total income (including the gain itself) in the disposal year. Plan disposal in a year with low other income to achieve 18% rather than 24% rate.
constraints.failure_mode: Applying a flat CGT rate without checking disposal-year income can understate CGT by up to 6 percentage points on large gains.

constraints.description: CLT limits use a rolling 7-year time-series, not an annual total. Gifts made 7+ years ago fall out of scope, freeing up NRB capacity. Sustained gifting of ~£46,500/year will breach the NRB when cumulated. Track as time-series array of {date, amount} tuples.
constraints.failure_mode: If tracked as cumulative total without time-series, the model never resets capacity as old gifts age out, and warns incorrectly of breaches.

constraints.description: At 3% inflation over 35 years, nominal spend target is 2.81× the year-1 figure (£80k/year → £225k/year nominal). Plans funded at 25 years often fail at 30–35 years. Always run a 35-year shadow projection alongside the user's selected plan_years.
constraints.failure_mode: Plans capped at 25 years create false confidence for users who may live to 90–95.

constraints.description: VCT subscription certificates frequently have missing, incorrect, or conflicting dates. If acquisition_date is missing, BPR and CGT-free clocks cannot be calculated. Flag missing dates; never assume BPR qualification when date is unknown.
constraints.failure_mode: Assuming BPR qualification overstates IHT exemption and may cause user to draw wrong assets first.

constraints.description: NEW in v1.1. If acquisition_cost is missing for any asset where CGT applies on draw (property, AIM, EIS pre-3-years, VCT pre-5-years), the CGT calculation cannot proceed. Flag the asset in output; never default to zero cost (this would massively overstate the CGT liability).
constraints.failure_mode: Missing cost data causes algorithm to either fail silently (treating as fully taxable) or default to zero (incorrect). Neither is acceptable.

constraints.description: CRITICAL FIX in v1.2. VCT shares are exempt from CGT on disposal under TCGA 1992 s.151A regardless of hold period. The v1.1 spec incorrectly stated 'CGT applies at standard rates before 5 years'. Early disposal (within qualifying period) triggers income tax relief clawback only — not CGT.
constraints.failure_mode: Applying CGT to early VCT disposals overstates disposal tax and distorts draw-order decisions. Could cause users to hold VCTs past optimal disposal point due to phantom CGT exposure.

constraints.description: CRITICAL FIX in v1.2. The previous ratio-based TFLS formula in v1.1 was mathematically unstable when partial crystallisations stacked across plan years. Correct formula: pcls = min(0.25 × gross_crystallisation, remaining_lsa). Applied per crystallisation event. remaining_lsa tracks as a running balance.
constraints.failure_mode: Incorrect formula understates or overstates tax-free component of pension draws in multi-event scenarios, misrepresenting income tax across entire plan.

constraints.description: CRITICAL (v1.2 addition, corrected v1.5). EIS investments may have deferred a CGT gain at entry. On disposal, the revived_deferred_gain crystallises as a CGT event — it is NOT added to income tax bands. It is stacked on top of other income to determine the applicable CGT rate (18%/24%). If revived_deferred_gain is missing, disposal-year CGT will be understated.
constraints.failure_mode: Missing revived_deferred_gain understates CGT in disposal year. Treating it as income tax (old error) overstates income tax and misrepresents the marginal rate applicable to other income in that year.

constraints.description: CRITICAL addition in v1.2. Using 2025/26 tax parameters for all future plan years is not safe for a 20–35 year plan. Tax rates, thresholds, and allowances change. The engine must use tax_parameter_schedule by plan year, and hold-flat for years beyond the schedule with a disclosed assumption.
constraints.failure_mode: Silent use of 2025/26 rates in year 15 of a plan produces precise-looking but wrong income tax and CGT calculations.

constraints.description: Relevant-property (discretionary) trusts face ten-year periodic charges (up to 6% of trust value) and exit charges on distributions. These are not modelled in v1. Users actively using discretionary trusts for IHT planning will have real trust costs that reduce the benefit compared to what the planner shows.
constraints.failure_mode: Planner overstates the net IHT benefit of discretionary trust strategy. Must be disclosed prominently wherever trust gifting outputs are shown.

constraints.description: CRITICAL addition in v1.4. VCTs are listed securities and do NOT qualify for Business Property Relief under IHTA 1984 s.105(3) and HMRC IHTM18113. Any spec, register field, or scoring logic that treats VCT as BPR-qualifying (is_iht_exempt=true based on BPR grounds) is wrong. VCT is in the estate at full value for IHT. The is_iht_exempt field must never be set to true for VCTs on BPR grounds. VCTs are also excluded from the £2.5M BPR/APR cap pool.
constraints.failure_mode: If VCT is incorrectly treated as BPR-qualifying, IHT bill will be materially understated for users with large VCT portfolios. Draw order will also be wrong — VCTs will be over-protected.

constraints.description: Mortgage liability treatment must be consistent throughout. Property value in IHT estate = gross market value minus deductible liabilities (per IHTA 1984 s.162). Net equity for funding purposes = gross disposal proceeds minus mortgage_balance minus disposal costs minus CGT. Charts may show gross asset value for portfolio visibility, but all tax and cashflow calculations must use net economics.
constraints.failure_mode: Using gross property value for IHT when there is a large mortgage balance overstates the taxable estate. Using gross proceeds for cashflow modelling overstates available funds in a disposal year.

conflict_resolution_priority_order.description: When constraints conflict in a given year, resolve in this strict order:
priority: 1. Cash reserve floor is absolute — never draw cash below cash_reserve under any circumstance.
2. Living cost spend target — fund from non-cash assets if cash floor is binding. Continue down non-cash draw queue.
3. Shortfall recording — if full queue exhausted and spend target still unmet, record shortfall. Do not override floors.
4. Legacy target — soft floor only. If spend target can only be met by breaching legacy target, fund spend and flag LEGACY_FLOOR_AT_RISK. Legacy target never blocks living cost spending.
5. Gift processing — gifts are processed after spend is confirmed funded. If cash is insufficient for both spend and gift, spend takes precedence.

warning_triggers.description: Defines when the flags array in the annual output is populated. Each flag has an id, severity (info/warning/error), and message.

triggers.condition: is_iht_exempt=false AND acquisition_date is within 2 years
triggers.message: Lot [label] has not yet reached 2-year BPR qualifying period. It is currently in your taxable estate.
Combined BPR/APR-qualifying assets approaching the proposed £2.5M cap. Assets above £2.5M will receive only 50% IHT relief under modelled 2026 rules.

triggers.condition: bpr_last_reviewed is more than 12 months ago
triggers.message: BPR qualification for [label] was last reviewed more than 12 months ago. Confirm with adviser that qualification still holds.
Cumulative discretionary trust gifts in last 7 years are approaching the £325,000 nil-rate band. Further gifts above this trigger immediate 20% IHT.
Cumulative discretionary trust gifts have exceeded the £325,000 nil-rate band. The excess is subject to immediate IHT at 20%.
This is the year the proposed pension IHT rule change takes effect (modelled assumption). Your undrawn pension pot is now included in the estimated IHT calculation.

This is the year the proposed BPR/APR cap takes effect (modelled assumption, announced December 2025). IHT relief on combined EIS+AIM qualifying assets above £2.5M per estate is now modelled at 50%. VCT holdings are unaffected by this cap — they are not BPR-qualifying.

triggers.condition: acquisition_date is null for VCT, EIS, or AIM lot
triggers.message: Acquisition date missing for [label]. BPR and CGT clock cannot be calculated. Asset excluded from BPR calculation — treated as in estate.

triggers.condition: acquisition_cost is null for any asset where CGT applies on draw
triggers.message: Acquisition cost missing for [label]. CGT cannot be calculated for this asset. Review register data before drawing from this asset.

This year's gift may require drawing capital rather than surplus income. NEFI exemption conditions may not be supported under our conservative test. Please review with an adviser before treating this gift as NEFI-exempt. No automatic reclassification applied.
Plan funds insufficient within 10 years. Consider reducing lifestyle level, delaying drawdown, or reviewing growth rate assumptions.
Shadow 35-year projection shown below. At 3% inflation, your spend target grows to [nominal_target_year_35] by year 35.
Your tax-free lump sum allowance is more than 75% used. Remaining TFLS: £[remaining]. Future pension draws above this amount are fully taxable.

triggers.condition: asset_class=eis AND relief_claimed_type includes cgt_deferral AND revived_deferred_gain is null
triggers.message: EIS lot [label] may have a revived deferred CGT gain that crystallises on disposal. revived_deferred_gain is missing — disposal-year CGT may be understated. This is a CGT event, not income tax. Check original investment records and EIS3 certificate.

triggers.condition: plan_year exceeds last entry in tax_parameter_schedule
triggers.message: Tax parameters for year [N] are held at [last scheduled year] values. Actual future rates may differ. This is a planning estimate, not a tax forecast.
Discretionary trusts may incur ten-year periodic charges and exit charges. These are not included in this calculation. Obtain separate adviser confirmation of ongoing trust costs.

output_schema.description: What the algorithm must return for each simulation year and for the plan summary

per_year.description: Sum of all asset values after draws and growth
Sum of BPR-qualifying asset values (is_iht_exempt=true AND bpr_qualifying_date passed), after applying BPR cap if toggle is on

IHT payable if user died at end of this year. Uses NRB-stacking formula: taxable_estate = total_portfolio_value - bpr_exempt (cap-adjusted) - pension (if pre-2027 or toggle off). available_nrb = nil_rate_band - clt_7yr_cumulative. iht = max(0, taxable_estate - available_nrb) × 0.40.
Total income tax paid on pension draws and other income this year
CGT paid on any disposals this year
Inflation-adjusted spend target for this year in nominal GBP
boolean — was the full spend target met from income + draws?
Amount by which spend target was not met (0 if fully met)

Total cash income generated without a draw decision: gross rental income received, gross interest received, gross dividends received (full amount before allowances), state_pension_annual (inflation-adjusted). Corresponds to cash_income track from step 2. Taxable treatment tracked separately — do not conflate with taxable_income_post_allowances.
Amount transferred out of estate this year via trust/gift
Rolling 7-year CLT total as of this year for NRB monitoring
Remaining TFLS/LSA allowance: tfls_lifetime_limit - tfls_used_amount. Tracked as running balance — decremented each time pcls is applied.
Alias for tfls_remaining — remaining Lump Sum Allowance available for future PCLS events.
Object: amount drawn from each asset class this year, with tax_paid per draw and pcls_applied for pension draws
Array of warning objects from warning_triggers — each with id, severity, message

plan_summary.description: boolean — is entire plan period funded without shortfall?
Year at which funds are first insufficient, if any. Null if fully funded.
Total nominal spend over plan period
Total income tax paid over plan period
Total CGT paid over plan period
Total transferred to trust/gifts over plan period
Portfolio value at end of plan period
Estimated IHT bill at end of plan period
estate_at_end - iht_at_end. The amount beneficiaries would actually receive. Always shown in estate summary card. (F009)
The user's specified net-of-IHT bequest goal. 0 if not set. (F004)
max(0, legacy_target - net_estate_after_iht). 0 if legacy target is met or not set. (F004)

IHT bill if no draws or gifts had ever been made — all assets grown at assumed_growth_rate for plan_years, no BPR clock resets, full BPR relief applied at death. Computed by calculateNoPlanIHT(). (F005)

iht_no_plan_baseline - iht_at_end. The IHT saving attributable to the plan. Negative means the plan increased IHT vs doing nothing (unusual but possible if growth assets were liquidated and cash held). (F005)
Always calculated — funded years on age-anchored shadow projection. Shadow horizon = max(plan_years, 35, 90 - current_age). Guarantees projection reaches at least age 90 and runs at least 35 years.

boolean. True when plan_confidence_level = LOW. When true, iht_saving_vs_no_plan and iht_no_plan_baseline are set to null and UI shows: 'IHT comparison requires complete asset data. Resolve data gaps to enable this comparison.'

drawdown_priority_scoring.description: Replaces the fixed four-strategy enum from v1.2. Each asset lot is scored on four dimensions; the composite score determines drawdown order. Higher score = draw LAST (more protected). Lower score = draw FIRST.

tax_efficiency.description: How much tax-shelter value does this asset retain if kept? Higher = more shelter = protect more.
tax_efficiency.convention: 10 = highest shelter remaining (protect). 1 = no shelter (draw first).

scoring_logic.note: Cash = 1 (draw first). Cash held in a cash-class wrapper produces interest taxed each year; it has no shelter value and depreciates in real terms. ISA = 10 (most valuable shelter to protect). Pension = 6 (partially sheltered but complex). VCT/EIS post qualifying period = 8–9 (CGT-free disposal, protect). Pre-qualifying = 3–4 (tax event on early disposal, still some shelter). AIM = 5 (CGT applies, partial shelter via BPR if qualifying). Property = 4 (CGT on disposal, no shelter but gains are real).

iht_reduction.description: How much does keeping this asset reduce the IHT bill? Higher = more IHT benefit from keeping = protect more.
iht_reduction.convention: 10 = maximum IHT benefit from keeping (protect). 1 = no IHT benefit from keeping (draw first).

scoring_logic.note: VCT = 1 on IHT dimension — VCTs are in-estate listed securities, no BPR qualification, zero IHT benefit from keeping. Cash/ISA/property = 1 (all in estate). Pension pre-2027 = 8 (currently outside estate — strong reason to keep). EIS/AIM fully qualifying within £2.5M cap = 10 (100% relief). EIS/AIM above cap = marginal score per bpr_marginal_relief_scoring.

preserve_growth.description: How much future growth will be foregone by drawing this asset now? Higher = more growth to lose = protect more.
preserve_growth.convention: 10 = highest assumed growth rate (protect to let compound). 1 = lowest growth rate (draw first).
preserve_growth.scoring_logic: Scores derived directly from each lot's assumed_growth_rate. Normalise across the register: score = round(assumed_growth_rate / max_growth_rate_in_register × 9) + 1. Cash at 1–2% growth → score 1–2. High-growth EIS/AIM at 10–15% → score 8–10. Property at 4–6% → score 4–5. Pension at 5–7% → score 5–6.

liquidity.description: How easy is this asset to access if the plan needs to change? Higher = more liquid = more optionality = protect more (counter-intuitive but correct: liquid assets give future flexibility, so we preserve them for emergency/optionality purposes).
liquidity.convention: IMPORTANT: This dimension is optionality-preservation, not draw-first. Higher liquidity score = keep for optionality. 10 = most liquid (preserve for flexibility). 1 = least liquid.

scoring_logic.note: Cash = 10 (instantly accessible — preserve for emergencies). Property = 1 (illiquid, indivisible — cannot use as buffer). Under PURE liquidity weight (1.0), cash is drawn LAST to preserve flexibility. This is appropriate behaviour for a 'keep cash accessible' preference. The combination with tax_efficiency (cash = 1) typically results in balanced treatment at equal weights.

liquidity.design_note: This dimension will create tension with tax_efficiency at equal weights — cash scores 10 on liquidity (protect) but 1 on tax_efficiency (draw first). At equal weighting (0.25 each), composite cash score = roughly 3.5, which correctly places cash below tax-sheltered assets but still makeable early. This is the intended balanced behaviour.

drawdown_priority_scoring.composite_score_formula: composite_protection_score = (tax_efficiency_score × w_tax) + (iht_reduction_score × w_iht) + (preserve_growth_score × w_growth) + (liquidity_score × w_liquidity). Weights are normalised (sum to 1.0). Sort ASCENDING by composite_protection_score to get draw order. Lowest composite score = drawn first. Highest = drawn last.
drawdown_priority_scoring.normalisation_rule: If weights sum to 0, default to equal weighting (0.25 each). Always normalise: w_i = raw_i / sum(raw). Normalise before scoring, not after.
edge_cases: Equal weights (0.25 each): balanced draw — cash typically scores ~3.5 composite (low tax shelter + high liquidity preservation offset). ISA scores ~7. EIS in-cap ~8.

iht_reduction weight = 1.0: all BPR-qualifying EIS/AIM lots (in-cap) score 10 on IHT — drawn last. VCT scores 1 on IHT — drawn early. Non-exempt assets (cash/ISA/property) score 1–2 on IHT — drawn earliest. Tax consequences are irrelevant at this extreme.
tax_efficiency weight = 1.0: ISA scores 10 (highest shelter, drawn last). Cash scores 1 (no shelter, drawn first). Pension scores ~6. VCT post-5yr scores 9 (CGT-exempt). EIS post-3yr scores 8.

liquidity weight = 1.0: cash scores 10 (preserved for emergencies, drawn last). Property scores 1 (most illiquid, drawn first — but constrained by indivisibility rule). This is counter-intuitive but correct: high liquidity score = preserve for optionality.
preserve_growth weight = 1.0: asset with highest assumed_growth_rate scores 10 (protect). Cash scores 1 (draw first). Score derived from lot's assumed_growth_rate relative to register max.
drawdown_priority_scoring.scoring_convention: PROTECTION SCORE — consistent across all dimensions. 10 = maximum protection, draw this asset LAST. 1 = minimum protection, draw this asset FIRST. Composite = weighted average protection score. Display order: high-to-low (most protected at top). Draw order: low-to-high (lowest score drawn first). Sort ascending by composite_protection_score to get draw order.

bpr_marginal_relief_scoring.description: Replaces binary BPR protection. IHT-reduction score is proportional to marginal relief benefit remaining — not a flat 10 for all qualifying lots.
algorithm: 1. Compute combined_bpr_qualifying_total across all EIS direct + AIM lots only where    is_iht_exempt=true and bpr_qualifying_date has passed. VCTs never included.
2. For each qualifying EIS/AIM lot, determine marginal position relative to bpr_full_relief_cap (£2.5M):
Partially above cap: pro-rate score between 5 and 10
Fully above cap (prior total already >= £2.5M): iht_reduction_score = 5
Pre-qualifying (BPR clock not yet complete): iht_reduction_score = 2
3. VCT lots: iht_reduction_score = 1 always (in-estate, no BPR)
bpr_marginal_relief_scoring.rationale: This ensures that once a user's BPR pool is already at £3M+, additional EIS/AIM above the cap gets a score of 5 not 10 — making it eligible for drawdown alongside non-BPR estate assets rather than always protected. Resolves the perverse case where over-cap BPR assets are treated as more protected than they actually are.

constraint_layer_rule.description: CRITICAL: The constraint layer always takes precedence over the scoring layer. Scoring only determines the ORDER of draws among assets that are eligible to be drawn. It cannot override hard constraints.
constraint_layer_includes: cash_reserve floor — never draw cash below this threshold regardless of score
property indivisibility — property is never scored as a partial annual draw; score only applies when a disposal decision has been made
wrapper rules — ISA-wrapped assets are taxed as ISA regardless of underlying asset class score
pension TFLS tracking — PCLS formula applied regardless of draw order
CLT limit — gifts processed before draws; trust limits are hard caps
legacy target — soft cap enforced after spend, surfaced as warning not a hard block
constraint_layer_rule.implementation_note: Run constraint checks FIRST to produce the eligible draw set for this year. THEN apply scoring to order the eligible set. Scoring never expands the eligible set — only orders within it.

action_plan_schema.description: Defines the structure of the year-by-year Action Plan output (F002). Translated from simulation perYear data into plain-English user-facing instructions.

output_per_year.is_key_year: boolean — true if this year meets any Key Year condition (see key_year_conditions)
output_per_year.actions: Array of action objects for this year

draw.description: Draw from a named asset class. Message: 'Draw £X from [asset]'. Includes tax note if income or CGT applies.

tax.description: Tax payment due. Message: 'Income tax £X due / CGT £X due on [asset] disposal'.

gift.description: Gifting event. Message: 'Transfer £X to [trust type]. Running CLT total: £Y of £325k NRB'.

milestone.description: Notable plan event. Examples: 'BPR qualifying date reached for [lot]', 'State pension commences', 'Pension enters IHT (2027 rule)'.

shortfall.description: Spend target not met. Message: 'Income shortfall of £X — assets insufficient to meet full spend target this year'.

depletion.description: Asset class reaches zero. Message: '[Asset class] fully depleted'. Surfaces as key year automatically.
