dispose.description: Asset disposal event (primarily property). Separate from draw. Message: 'Sell [asset]: gross proceeds £X'. Always followed by tax and transfer_to_cash events.

transfer_to_cash.description: Net disposal proceeds transferred to cash balance. Message: 'Net proceeds £X transferred to cash reserve (available for future years)'.

surplus_carried_forward.description: Disposal proceeds exceed this year's spend target. Message: 'Surplus £X carried forward in cash — funds year [N+1] and beyond'.

band_management.description: Engine stopped pension draw at tax band threshold. Message: 'Pension draw capped at £X to stay within basic-rate band. Remaining spend sourced from [next asset]'.

driver_note.description: One-line explanation of why this year's draw order changed vs prior year. Generated whenever the primary draw source changes. Message: e.g. 'Cash reserve floor prevented further cash draw this year' / 'BPR clock completed for [lot]' / '2027 pension IHT rule now active'.

key_year_conditions: Any year with a draw source change (different asset class drawn vs prior year)
Any year with a depletion event
Any year a BPR qualifying date is reached

summary_totals.total_drawn: Sum of all draws over plan period
summary_totals.total_tax_paid: Income tax + CGT over plan period

multi_asset_year_rule.description: When more than 2 asset classes are drawn in a single year, the Action Plan switches from individual draw lines to a grouped summary plus breakdown.
multi_asset_year_rule.grouped_format: 'Fund this year from: [income £X], [ISA £Y], [pension £Z]' followed by collapsible line-by-line breakdown.
multi_asset_year_rule.always_separate: Tax events, property disposal events, and gift events are always shown as separate explicit action lines — never merged into grouped draw summaries.

property_disposal_year_event_order.description: Mandatory event sequence in any year where a property disposal occurs.
required_sequence: 2. debt_repayment [if mortgage_balance > 0]: 'Repay mortgage £Y — net equity before tax £Z'
3. tax_provision: 'CGT provision £A (gain £B less £3k exempt, at [18/24]% rate)'
5. draw: 'Fund year [N] spending from disposal proceeds £D'
6. surplus_carried_forward [if applicable]: 'Surplus £E carried to cash — funds year [N+1] onwards'
property_disposal_year_event_order.rationale: Without this ordering, users read gross proceeds as spending cash. The chain makes gross/debt/tax/net/carry-forward explicit.

known_simplifications_in_v1.description: Areas where v1 makes simplifying assumptions. These must be disclosed to users in the UI.
simplifications: Modelled as single UK-resident individual. Spouse/civil partner NRB transfers, transferable nil-rate band (up to £650k combined), and joint ownership are not modelled.
Residential Nil-Rate Band (RNRB: £175,000) not modelled — IHT shown may be higher than actual if main residence passes to direct descendants. UI note required wherever IHT is shown.
Defined benefit pensions treated as flat income stream. Capital value, commutation, and survivor benefits not modelled.
State pension excluded by default (optional field available). Omitting it slightly overstates asset consumption.
CGT disposal-year optimisation not automatically selected — algorithm flags good disposal years but does not reschedule disposals across the plan.
Sequence-of-returns risk not modelled — flat growth rate assumptions. Monte Carlo deferred to v2.
Care costs not modelled — a major tail risk for users aged 80+.
Divorce or relationship change not modelled.
Currency risk on overseas assets not modelled.
HMRC challenge risk on NEFI gifts not modelled — algorithm flags potential NEFI eligibility risk but classification remains with user/adviser.

BPR on trading businesses, agricultural property (APR), and woodland relief not modelled. BPR logic covers EIS direct investments and AIM shares only. VCTs are explicitly excluded from the BPR pool — they are listed and do not qualify for Business Property Relief.
April 2026 BPR cap and April 2027 pension IHT changes are modelled assumptions based on announced proposals, not enacted law. Presented as toggleable scenarios with disclosure banners.
Non-domiciled status, offshore structures, and corporate ownership not modelled — UK resident individual only.
LTA protections and pre-April-2024 crystallisation history not modelled — LSA treated as fully unused unless tfls_used_amount is populated at onboarding.
Lot disposal for quoted multi-lot holdings uses average-cost approximation. Full HMRC share matching (TCGA 1992 s.104) not implemented. VCT/EIS lots are always treated individually — no averaging.
Discretionary trust ten-year periodic charges and exit charges not modelled. Trust outputs show entry and death-interaction effects only.
Post-2027 pension IHT outputs show estate-level IHT only. Beneficiary-side income tax on inherited pension drawdown is not modelled.
Tax parameters beyond the last tax_parameter_schedule entry are held flat at the most recent known values. Future rate changes will differ.
EIS deferred gains are captured where revived_deferred_gain field is populated. If missing from register data, disposal-year CGT may be understated.

Legacy target conflict resolution: if meeting living costs would cause the projected net estate to fall below the legacy target, spend is funded and LEGACY_FLOOR_AT_RISK warning fires. Legacy target influences sequencing and warnings — it does not hard-block living-cost draws.

Cash buffer conflict resolution: when cash_reserve floor prevents drawing from cash, the engine continues to eligible non-cash assets in normal priority order. Shortfall is recorded only if the full eligible queue is exhausted.

BPR-qualifying lots above the £2.5M cap still receive 50% IHT relief (not 0%). The scoring system treats them as fully protected when they are only partially protected. This is a v1 known limitation — see BPR_ABOVE_CAP_NO_MARGINAL_BENEFIT warning.

data_confidence_framework.description: Every simulation run must produce a plan_confidence_level alongside outputs. This prevents polished-looking plans from masking weak data quality.

levels.HIGH: All critical fields present: acquisition_date, acquisition_cost, wrapper_type, tfls_used_amount, is_iht_exempt confirmed. No stale BPR reviews. All VCT/EIS lots have relief_claimed_type. revived_deferred_gain populated where cgt_deferral used.
levels.MEDIUM: One or two estimation fields missing: e.g. revived_deferred_gain null on one lot, bpr_last_reviewed stale on one lot. Outputs still reliable but individual calculations may have uncertainty.
levels.LOW: Core tax fields missing: acquisition_cost null on a property or AIM lot, acquisition_date missing on VCT/EIS lot, wrapper_type defaulted, tfls_used_amount = 0 with no confirmation. Outputs unreliable for tax-sensitive decisions.

MAY_UNDERSTATE_DISPOSAL_TAX: missing revived_deferred_gain on EIS cgt_deferral lot

data_confidence_framework.ui_requirement: Plan confidence level must be shown prominently on every output view — not buried in warnings panel. LOW confidence must trigger a banner: 'This plan has data gaps that affect tax calculations. Review highlighted fields before making decisions.'

blocking_checklist.description: Shown before first results when plan_confidence_level = LOW. User must explicitly fix or acknowledge each blocker.

blockers.plain_english: We don't know how much tax-free cash you've already taken from pensions.
blockers.impact: Future pension tax calculations may be overstated.
blockers.action: Enter your cumulative TFLS amount, or confirm it's zero.

blockers.plain_english: One or more asset wrapper types were not confirmed.
blockers.impact: Wrong wrapper = wrong tax treatment throughout.
blockers.action: Review and confirm the wrapper type for each flagged asset.

blockers.plain_english: A VCT holding is flagged as IHT-exempt. VCTs don't qualify for Business Relief.
blockers.action: Remove IHT-exempt flag from VCT lots.

blockers.plain_english: Acquisition cost is missing for an asset where capital gains tax applies.
blockers.impact: CGT on disposal cannot be calculated.
blockers.action: Add acquisition cost from contract note or adviser records.

blockers.plain_english: An EIS holding used CGT deferral at entry but the deferred gain amount is missing.
blockers.impact: Tax on EIS disposal will be understated.
blockers.action: Check your EIS3 certificate or original investment records.

blocking_checklist.ui_behaviour: Show each blocker with Fix now / Proceed with warning buttons. Plan results shown with LOW confidence banner if user proceeds without fixing.

ux_and_onboarding_requirements.description: Product and UX requirements derived from v1.3 reviewer feedback. These govern how the planner is presented, not just what it computes.

user_must_confirm: This tool produces planning estimates, not regulated financial advice.
Outputs are based on current assumptions. Proposed rule changes (BPR cap, pension IHT) are modelled scenarios, not enacted law.
Disposal and gifting decisions should be reviewed with a qualified adviser before acting.
first_run_acknowledgement.gate: User cannot view simulation outputs until acknowledgement is confirmed. One-time per plan, not per session.

onboarding_flow_order: Step 1: Who is this plan for? (confirm: single UK individual, age, family context note — joint/spouse not modelled)
Step 2: What matters most? (preset picker: Spend well / Balance inheritance / Protect legacy / Tax efficient)
Step 3: Asset register — pre-filled from Unlock register. Show completeness score immediately.

Step 4: Critical field review — show 7 fields that can poison the model if wrong: pension TFLS history, wrapper types, acquisition costs, acquisition dates, relief_claimed_type, revived_deferred_gain, mortgage balance. Require explicit confirm-or-skip-with-warning per field.
Step 5: Assumptions — inflation, state pension, annual gifts, scenario toggles. Advanced options (legacy target, cash buffer, weight sliders) behind 'Advanced planning controls' accordion.
Step 6: Confidence check — show plan_confidence_level before results load. LOW confidence triggers mandatory review prompt.

input_labelling.description: Every user-facing input must be badged by type so users understand what they control vs what the engine enforces.
input_labelling.hard_rule_badge: Cash buffer floor, property indivisibility, TFLS cap, pension band limits
input_labelling.soft_preference_badge: Legacy target, weighted priority sliders, preset profiles
input_labelling.scenario_assumption_badge: 2026 BPR cap toggle, 2027 pension IHT toggle
input_labelling.planning_estimate_badge: Growth rates, inflation rate, state pension amount

liquidity.live_example: At this setting, cash is preserved as emergency flexibility — spending may come from ISA or other assets before cash is drawn. This is intentional.
liquidity.counter_intuitive_note: High score = PROTECT cash (draw it last). Low score = draw cash first. Explain this in the UI — users expect 'accessible' to mean draw-first, but the correct financial behaviour is the opposite.

slider_human_readable_labels.note: Show display label large. Show technical label small below. Show slider tooltip: 'Moving this slider changes the priority order of asset draws. It does not guarantee this outcome.'

constraint_visibility.when_cash_buffer_binds: Show banner: 'Your cash reserve floor (£X) is limiting draws this year. Remaining spend sourced from other assets.'
constraint_visibility.when_legacy_target_at_risk: Show banner: 'Your plan may not meet your legacy target of £X. Current projected net estate: £Y. Consider reducing spend or adjusting the target.'
constraint_visibility.when_plan_operationally_fragile: Show warning: 'This plan is mathematically fundable but operationally tight — it depends heavily on [illiquid asset disposals / concentrated EIS exits]. Consider holding additional liquid reserves.'

action_plan_language.disclaimer_per_year: Each year entry should include a small footnote: 'Illustrative — not financial advice. Review with an adviser before acting.'

operational_realism_warning.trigger: When >60% of a future year's spend target is modelled to come from a single illiquid asset disposal (property or EIS), or when liquid assets fall below 12 months of spend target
operational_realism_warning.message: This plan is mathematically fundable but operationally tight. Year [N] relies heavily on a single asset disposal. Real-world timing, market conditions, and tax may differ.

items: Spouse/civil partner exemptions and transferable nil-rate band not modelled — estate figures may be overstated for married users.
Residential Nil-Rate Band (£175k) not modelled — estate figures may be overstated if main residence passes to direct descendants.
DB pension income modelled as flat stream — commutation, survivor benefits, and capital value not modelled.
Discretionary trust ten-year and exit charges not modelled.

dividend_rate_update_2026_27.description: Tax update from reviewer: 2026/27 dividend rates changed.
dividend_rate_update_2026_27.note: Add 2026/27 entry to tax_parameter_schedule. Dividend allowance remains £500. All other headline rates (PA, basic/higher thresholds, CGT 18/24%, LSA, AA, MPAA, ISA allowance) held as per 2025/26.

vct_relief_rate_update_april_2026.description: Tax update from reviewer: VCT upfront income tax relief reduces from 30% to 20% from 6 April 2026 for new subscriptions.
vct_relief_rate_update_april_2026.impact_on_engine: For VCT lots subscribed on or after 6 April 2026: tax_relief_claimed = 20% × subscription_amount. For pre-6-April-2026 lots: clawback basis remains 30%. The asset schema must capture subscription_date to determine applicable relief rate.
vct_relief_rate_update_april_2026.impact_on_clawback: Early disposal clawback for post-April-2026 VCTs = 20% × original subscription (not 30%). Engine must use the rate applicable at time of subscription.
vct_relief_rate_update_april_2026.note: This does not affect investor annual subscription limits (£200k remains). It affects: tax_relief_claimed field, clawback calculation on early disposal, and net cost basis.

screen_1.title: What this planner does (and doesn't)
screen_1.body: This is a planning tool for a single UK individual. It models today's tax rules plus two announced future scenarios. It is not regulated financial advice.
not_modelled: Spouse or civil partner exemptions and transferable nil-rate band
Residential nil-rate band (£175k) — your IHT estimate may be overstated if main residence passes to direct descendants
Care costs — a significant tail risk from age 80+
Defined benefit pension commutation, survivor rights, or capital value
Offshore structures, corporate ownership, or non-UK domicile
Discretionary trust ten-year and exit charges
screen_1.required_checkbox: I understand this is a planning estimate, not personal financial advice.

conditional_banners.if_property_and_children: Your IHT figures may be overstated — Residential Nil-Rate Band (£175k) is not modelled.
conditional_banners.if_joint_assets_or_spouse: This planner models one individual. Spouse/CP exemptions and transferable nil-rate band are not included.
conditional_banners.if_db_pension: Your defined benefit pension is modelled as a flat income stream — commutation, survivor benefits, and capital value are not included.

screen_2.title: Your target and time horizon
left_inputs: Lifestyle level — Modest / Comfortable / Generous / Unlimited (descriptions from spec)
Plan years — numeric, default to max(35, 90 - current_age)
Current age — required, 55–90, hint: 'Used only to anchor the long-term projection'
right_live_preview: Total planned lifetime spending vs current portfolio value (no tax — gut-check only)
Note: 'We always also test your plan to age 90'

screen_3.title: How do you want to plan?
screen_3.instruction: Pick a planning style — you can fine-tune later.

cards.label: Spend well
cards.caption: Prioritise income today. Inheritance is a bonus.

cards.label: Balanced
cards.caption: Sustainable income with a meaningful inheritance.

cards.label: Protect my legacy
cards.caption: Spend comfortably, but design the plan around a large estate.

screen_3.first_run_results: funded_years + net_estate_after_iht + plan_confidence_level badge only. No charts on first run if confidence is LOW.

simulation_inputs_schema.description: Complete input schema for runSimulation(). All fields added in v1.3 are marked NEW.

annual_income_target.description: Annual net income required in today's money. Inflation-adjusted each year.

current_age.description: User's current age. Range 55–90 in v1. Shadow horizon = max(plan_years, 35, 90 - current_age).

state_pension_annual.description: Annual state pension income (nominal). Reduces required draws.

priority_weights.description: Four-dimension drawdown priority weights. Must sum to 1.0 (auto-normalised). See drawdown_priority_scoring section.

cash_reserve.description: Minimum cash balance across all cash-class assets at all times. Engine enforces as hard floor — never draws cash below this threshold. If spend target cannot be met without breaching floor, the shortfall is recorded but cash is not drawn.

legacy_target.description: Net-of-IHT bequest goal. Engine uses this to influence draw sequencing and surface LEGACY_FLOOR_AT_RISK when projected net estate falls below this figure. Does NOT hard-block living-cost spending. Legacy target is a soft optimisation preference — spend target always takes precedence.

calculateNoPlanIHT.description: Computes IHT as at end of plan_years if the user made no draws and no gifts at all — the 'do nothing' baseline. Used to calculate iht_saving_vs_no_plan. (F005)
algorithm: 1. Grow each lot at its assumed_growth_rate for plan_years years (compound, no draws).
2. No gifts, no CLTs, no PETs — available_nrb = full nil_rate_band.
3. Apply BPR relief at plan_years using the same bpr_full_relief_cap and toggle logic as the main simulation.
4. Apply apply_2027_pension_iht toggle: if ON, pension value included in estate.
5. Compute IHT: taxable = total - bpr_exempt - pension(if excluded). iht = max(0, taxable - nil_rate_band) * iht_rate.
calculateNoPlanIHT.open_question_for_review: Should the no-plan baseline apply the 2026/2027 scenario toggles in the same way as the main plan? If the toggles are OFF in the main plan, it seems inconsistent to apply them in the baseline. Current implementation: toggles applied identically in both main plan and baseline so the comparison is apples-to-apples.

scoreAssetForDrawdown.description: Scores a single asset lot against the four priority dimensions using the current year's priority_weights. Returns composite score — lower = draw sooner. (F001, F007)
scoreAssetForDrawdown.returns: number — protection score from 1–10. Higher = protect (draw last). Lower = draw first. Sort ascending by this score to get draw order.

LEGACY_FLOOR_AT_RISK.condition: legacy_target > 0 AND (estate_at_end - projected_iht) < legacy_target AND spend_met = true
LEGACY_FLOOR_AT_RISK.message: The plan is drawing down assets to fund living costs in a way that will not meet your legacy target of £X. Net estate at plan end is projected at £Y (after IHT). Consider reducing spend, increasing gifting efficiency, or adjusting the legacy target.

CASH_BUFFER_CONSTRAINING_DRAW.condition: In any year: spend_met = false AND cash assets are above zero but below cash_reserve
CASH_BUFFER_CONSTRAINING_DRAW.message: Your cash buffer floor (£X) prevented full draw from cash this year. If you need to fund the shortfall, consider drawing from another asset class or reducing the cash buffer.

BPR_ABOVE_CAP_NO_MARGINAL_BENEFIT.condition: apply_2026_bpr_cap=true AND combined_bpr_qualifying_total > bpr_full_relief_cap AND asset being scored has is_iht_exempt=true
BPR_ABOVE_CAP_NO_MARGINAL_BENEFIT.message: Your BPR-qualifying assets (£X) exceed the proposed £2.5M cap. Assets above the cap receive only 50% IHT relief. Drawing some BPR-qualifying assets above the cap may be IHT-neutral — consider reviewing the draw order with an adviser.

PCLS_HISTORY_UNCERTAIN.message: You are over 60 with a significant pension and no TFLS history recorded. If you have previously taken tax-free cash from any pension scheme, tfls_used_amount must be updated or future PCLS calculations will be overstated. Please confirm your TFLS history.

MPAA_STATUS_INCONSISTENT.condition: mpaa_triggered = true AND recent pension contributions suggest contribution rate > £10,000/year
MPAA_STATUS_INCONSISTENT.message: MPAA appears to be triggered for this pension, but contribution levels may exceed the £10,000 MPAA limit. Please confirm MPAA status with your pension provider.

LIQUIDITY_RISK.message: Liquid assets cover less than 2 years of planned spending. The plan may be operationally fragile. Consider maintaining a minimum liquid buffer before drawing from illiquid assets.

VCT_IHT_EXEMPT_FLAG_INCORRECT.message: VCT holdings cannot qualify for Business Property Relief (HMRC IHTM18113). The is_iht_exempt flag on this VCT lot appears incorrectly set. VCTs are in your estate at full value for IHT. Please review and correct this in your asset register.

ISA_GIFT_DESTROYS_SHELTER.condition: gift amount funded from ISA withdrawal in this year
ISA_GIFT_DESTROYS_SHELTER.message: This year's gift requires an ISA withdrawal. ISA withdrawals permanently lose their tax-free shelter (unless flexible ISA). Consider funding gifts from unwrapped cash first to preserve ISA compounding.

WRAPPER_TYPE_REVIEW_REQUIRED.condition: any lot where wrapper_type was defaulted rather than explicitly confirmed at onboarding
WRAPPER_TYPE_REVIEW_REQUIRED.message: Wrapper type for [asset] was not explicitly confirmed at onboarding and may have been defaulted. Incorrect wrapper type causes wrong tax treatment throughout the plan. Please review.
